In this meetup, we’ll dive into the latest tools and ideas we’ve been shipping — what they unlock, how they fit together, and why we built them the way we did.

This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what’s next for the analytics engineering stack.

Whether you’re curious about what’s new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions — you’re in the right place.

🤝Organizer: The Information Lab
🏠Venue Host: The Information Lab
Floor 1, 25 Watling Street,
London EC4M 9BR
🍕Catering: bites and refreshments

📝Agenda

5:30 - 6:10 | Check in/Registration (40 min)
6:10 - 6:15 | Welcome Remarks (5 min)
6:15 - 6:45 | Presentation 1: “For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs” by Richard Persaud (Majors Solutions Architect @ dbt Labs) + Q&A (30 min)
6:50 - 8:30 | Food + Networking Reception

Event Details:
To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation:** https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Doors open from 5:30 onward.
We have an occupancy limit so if you can no longer attend please update your RSVP accordingly.

Upon arrival if the door is not open feel free to ring the reception. Here we will be checking in attendees and guiding them up to the first floor where the event will take place.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/ and join the #local-ldn channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/)

***

dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 50,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. 🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

In case you missed it, dbt Labs is hosting Coalesce 2022 from October 17 - 21. It is the annual conference dedicated to advancing the practice of analytics engineering. Coalesce offers data practitioners an opportunity to deepen their knowledge and network. You can choose your own adventure including in-person, online, and hybrid experiences.

Coalesce Online (FREE) | October 17-21, 2022. The online experience that you know and love. Connect with data practitioners around the globe in sessions designed for North America, APAC, EMEA and LATAM regions. Catch live stream sessions from Coalesce New Orleans, interact with the community and speakers via Slack, and attend exclusively virtual sessions.
Coalesce New Orleans | October 17-21, 2022 | Located at the Hyatt Regency, New Orleans. Our first time together, in person! Attend a presentation, workshop, or panel discussion. Participate in activities, a hackathon, intimate discussions, and social activities. Meet community members and partners while exploring the history and culture of New Orleans.
Coalesce Online + | London, 20 October 2022 | Sydney, 21 October 2022. A one-day event taking place in London and Sydney, complementing Coalesce Online. This will include tech talks, hosted discussions, an Executive keynote and a post-event networking session.

⚠️Please note that Coalesce 2022 won't be hosted on Meetup.com.

🎤 Reporting on dbt usage
🤝 Juliusz Wiatrak, Senior BI Developer, Farfetch

🎤 CI/CD pipeline using dbt, Docker, and Jenkins
🤝 Ben Balde, Data Engineer, Simply Business

In this presentation, Ben will dive into:

how to use Docker to host a dbt image shared by users/systems
how running dbt in a container has made it easier for Simply Business to onboard new users
how to create a robust Jenkins CI/CD pipeline by building automated dbt tests that run against production data and reduce the risk of broken code reaching the production database

🤝 Andrea Fabry, Senior Data Engineer, VTS

How do you weigh the pros and cons of introducing a new tool to your data stack? VTS, a proptech unicorn with over 400 global employees, is currently running proof of value with dbt. In this talk, Andrea will share how her team set up the project to demonstrate the value dbt adds by helping data engineers write modular, performant, well-tested, and well-documented code.

6pm: Doors open, networking, pizza, drinks.

The event is generously hosted by Simply Business at their London offices.

About Simply Business

Here at Simply Business, we're obsessed with making the process of finding insurance as simple as it can be for small businesses, with no hint of compromise on choice, quality or cost.

So far, we've created a unique, digital marketplace for SME insurance which features dozens of valuable insurers and, in the process, have challenged a traditional industry – taking us from an ambitious start-up to a half-billion dollar company.

Simply Business believes in using technology to delight, amaze and give back real value to our customers, our people, and the wider community. We’re proud to be an empowered and committed team working on a global platform renowned for its excellence.

About dbt

For anyone unfamiliar with dbt: dbt is the T in ELT. It lets you organize, cleanse, denormalize, filter, rename, and pre-aggregate the raw data in your warehouse so that it’s ready for analysis. More info at https://getdbt.com.

Details

Come mingle with dbt users and members of the London analytics community!

We’ve got a great line-up of speakers for our inaugural event, which I’m sure will lead to great discussion with members of the community.

In this first event, we are going to focus on how the analytics stack has evolved leading to the emergence of dbt, how businesses are leverages dbt to handle the complexity of their data, what’s coming in the future for dbt, and more.

The agenda is as follows:

6pm: Doors open, networking, pizza, drinks.

7pm: Talks starts! They are:

• How analytics stacks have evolved and an introduction to dbt, Dom Sventickas from Growth Street
• Managing model quality and complexity in dbt, Colleen Mahon from Tails.com
• Thinking probabilistically: helping your business making better decisions, Dylan Baker (freelance consultant)
• The future of dbt, Drew Banin from Fishtown Analytics

8:15pm: Talks finish, Q&A.

8:30pm: Further drinks and networking

The event is going to be generously hosted by Growth Street at their London offices and sponsored by Snowflake.
